So for every person who starts a diet, there's 10 people who are good at starting diet as soon as The next event occurs and I'm gonna start right after my cruise. I'm gonna start right after the holidays I'm gonna start right after my vacation. I'm gonna start right after there's a right after Point at which I'm gonna do this that I'm not gonna do now, right? So now we got to get agreement somehow that personal timing is either There's only two ways to approach this one. It either has to be made advantageous Or it has to be made so urgent That bad personal timing is trumped by the urgency. There's only two things that we can do to them So once we understand their personal timing issues So I don't want to steal his presentation, but at lunch Craig Simpson was telling me about the use of a particular list in which they discovered profound seasonality The list responsive at one time of year and utterly unresponsive at other times of year This now gives us actually three choices. One's a tactical mechanical choice. One is ignore the list Except for the three months out of the year when it's exceptionally responsive and then be sure you mail the live and be Jesus out of it While you can so there's a tactical response in that case because there's other lists if it was our only market If it was who we sell to and we have no one else to sell to then that tactical option doesn't solve our problem So now we don't have the option of just selling to them three months out of the year. They're all we got right So now we got to do one or two things we got to figure out what all what their personal timing excuses are Which in this particular case, which he'll tell you about tomorrow, they're obvious and they're shared Their personal but they're shared by every one of the persons in this market So now we either got to convince them that what they think is Disadvantages timing is actually ideal timing or We've got to get their agreement that The urgency of action is so great That we acknowledge the fact that the personal timing is disadvantageous, but you got to act anyway So You've all been in hotels probably I hate to jinx this but you've all been in hotels probably when the fire alarm goes off right So some of us Ignore it no matter when If you've traveled a lot you get to the point just like a car alarm going off in a parking lot now doesn't It all that doesn't make a dog park everybody else ignores it well fire alarms and hotels the most seasoned travelers of us at all real road warriors Particularly and I've done my own little survey so I've asked his egg. I asked Brian. I asked Tommy We all do the same thing. We just say screw it, you know The odds are we're not going to get burned up and if we do we do but by God I'm not going out and standing in the parking lot right so So everybody leaves but us, you know Some people though are very situational about it Hey if they're dressed And they were going down to dinner anyway, they'll flee the building If it's three o'clock in the morning and they're in their underwear and they're trying to sleep I'll take my chances. All right, so they're situational about it, right? So the only way you're going to get about three o'clock in the morning is if you're able to convince them the damn thing is on fire Then they'll move so those are our choices Finally agreement 11 is Personal appropriateness now this is interesting because it gets back to belief systems Religious and otherwise Okay, so somebody can agree to everything leading up to this and still feel That Not for them All right, they can agree to everything else. So the example I give you in the book is When Mary says she doesn't want a house that that big or fancy of a house because Whatever would we do with all that space and gee it's going to take a lot to keep it clean and all that in most cases What she's really expressing is either disbelief and personal attainability So I don't want to want it because I can't have it or it's inappropriate for me to have it all right, and so Appropriateness gets all the way back to people's beliefs about money and success and ambition And many many people will acknowledge will make all of these other 11 agreements and then they Finally get stopped at is this really right for me? Do I deserve it? Is it appropriate for me to have it? Is it ethical for me to have it? Is it okay for me to have it? What will other people think of me? What will the neighbors think? Etc so, you know a small example of that is Visible big ticket purchases By the affluent were affected in recent years not because their inability to buy them or their lack of desire to have them But because they didn't want to come drive home in the big new Lexus and park it in the driveway when across the street from them They knew that guy was getting laid off and two doors down Somebody's house was in foreclosure, so they stopped at it was just inappropriate to do it and Be seen with it at this moment in time, right? And so it is sense although we're talking about a car now They got all the way through all the other agreements, but they stopped at number 11 and they just decided it was not You know appropriate and so We ultimately have to deal with that So now we've talked a lot about where they are And what's going on in their point pointy little heads? And in many cases You have probably Begun to think about that differently Than you did yesterday and think about their mindset and where they are and where we are starting with them So now we'll talk about some of the key elements of a opportunity positioned and language presentation of anything That is just that is designed to help counter These things going on in their head
